prompt: Create a vertical heraldic banner for Mithrandir, Gandalf as pilgrim of the West and keeper of secret fire: a luminous white ring or halo above a subtle wandering staff and star-lit path motif, evoking wisdom, warding, foresight, mercy, Rivendell and Lothlorien, with a noble but quiet sacred authority. Match the existing RetroLOTR alignment banner family: vertical standard, clear central emblem, restrained fantasy heraldry, crisp silhouette, readable at small UI size, transparent or isolated background, no text, no watermark, no modern graphic design. Use the style spirit of these existing banners: banner_white_ring, banner_blue_star_harp, banner_elven_leaf, banner_white_stars_wings, banner_blue_white_tree. Leader context: Gandalf is the grey pilgrim, a wanderer of hidden roads and quiet counsel. He walks the edge between shadow and hope, guiding the small and the humble because he knows their courage can outlast any sword. He trusts in patience, in wisdom earned by travel, and in the spark that lights when others have given in to fear. The Shire lies under his watch, and the first steps of his journey begin among its green lanes, where he gathers trusted companions, listens for the first uneasy rumors, and searches old ground for relics that have waited too long. His path starts in quiet places, but it will not remain quiet. Variant context: Mithrandir, pilgrim of the West, passes like an old song through Rivendell, Lothlorien, and the hidden paths where the Wise still keep watch against the ancient Shadow. In him abide memory, mercy, and the secret fire set against a darkness older than the kingdoms of Men, and where he treads, veils are lifted and the designs of the Enemy are read before they ripen to ruin. Under Mithrandir, the game turns toward revelation, warding, foresight, and spellcraft wielded with patient precision rather than open might.

prompt: Create a vertical heraldic banner for Saruman the White in his stately Orthanc persona: a stark pale tower rising above precise rings or ordered lines, elegant and severe, suggesting eloquence, mastery, lore, manipulation and cold authority rather than open brutality. Match the existing RetroLOTR alignment banner family: vertical standard, clear central emblem, restrained fantasy heraldry, crisp silhouette, readable at small UI size, transparent or isolated background, no text, no watermark, no modern graphic design. Use the style spirit of these existing banners: banner_brown_tower, banner_white_hand, banner_white_blue_shield, banner_brown_gold_ring, banner_white_ring. Leader context: Saruman is the master of Orthanc, once the chief of the wise, now the architect of his own dominion.His voice bends courts, his mind measures every weakness, and his ambition refuses to wait for permission. He builds in silence, gathering strength behind fair words, and he intends to remake the world in the image of his will. His design begins with a careful reach into the courts of Rohan, the securing of a pliant voice, and the quiet unearthing of power long buried. From Orthanc he turns influence into force and raises the first ranks of his war. Variant context: Saruman the White stands in the majesty of Orthanc, chief of the Istari, master of lore, ring-craft, and the long devices of the Wise. His voice is a weapon finer than steel, entering the counsels of kings and bending proud hearts with honeyed reason until obedience seems wisdom and surrender seems peace. This variant favors influence, manipulation, and command exercised with polished restraint.

prompt: Create a vertical heraldic banner for Sauron as the Dark Eye: a lidless burning eye over black battlements and volcanic shadow, sleepless, oppressive and watchful, expressing surveillance, dread, Nazgul terror, the Black Gate and a suffocating dominion that sees all. Match the existing RetroLOTR alignment banner family: vertical standard, clear central emblem, restrained fantasy heraldry, crisp silhouette, readable at small UI size, transparent or isolated background, no text, no watermark, no modern graphic design. Use the style spirit of these existing banners: banner_eye, banner_dark_eye, banner_mt_doom, banner_nazgul, banner_fire_iron. Leader context: Sauron is the Dark Lord returned, a will of iron that has never forgotten its claim. From Barad-dur he bends the lands through fear and patience, drawing all things toward a single purpose. He does not need love or loyalty, only obedience, and he will take it by force or by dread. The hunt begins in the shadow of Minas Morgul, where the trail is secured and a relic of power is drawn from cursed ground. From there the march moves toward the Black Gate, where the first disciplined hosts take shape under a hard command. Variant context: The Dark Eye burns above Barad-dur like a star of malice that never sets, sleepless over Gorgoroth, the Black Gate, and the dead roads beneath Ephel Duath. It is dread made watchful, a will that peers through palantiri, dreams, and trembling rumor until no secrecy feels safe and every hidden movement seems already betrayed. This variant is built on exposure, fear, and a suffocating dominion that narrows the enemy's every choice.

prompt: Create a vertical heraldic banner for Sauron the Deceiver, Annatar and Lord of Gifts: a fair-seeming but sinister emblem, such as a beautiful crowned flame, forged ring, or elegant star corrupted from within, expressing seduction, false gifts, smith-craft, Numenorean pride and hidden domination before open war. Match the existing RetroLOTR alignment banner family: vertical standard, clear central emblem, restrained fantasy heraldry, crisp silhouette, readable at small UI size, transparent or isolated background, no text, no watermark, no modern graphic design. From Barad-dur he bends the lands through fear and patience, drawing all things toward a single purpose. He does not need love or loyalty, only obedience, and he will take it by force or by dread. The hunt begins in the shadow of Minas Morgul, where the trail is secured and a relic of power is drawn from cursed ground.
